# UC-18.4.12 · Nexus Dashboard Insights Alert Noise and Category Mix

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Fault, Compliance, Operations, Data Quality &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how many Nexus Insights alerts pile up by category and when one kind suddenly dominates the mix, so your team can trim repeat alarms without missing a real fabric problem.*

---

## Description

Measures hourly Nexus Dashboard Insights alert and anomaly volume by normalized category (compliance, capacity, configuration, change, connectivity, environmental) across cisco:ndi:alerts and cisco:ndi:anomalies while correlating cisco:ndfc:fabric_status, cisco:ndfc:audit, and cisco:ndfc:syslog context to expose concentration spikes, duplicate fan-out, and controller-plane churn that masquerade as multi-fabric failures.

## Value

Operators distinguish a single-root-cause storm from true multi-domain regression so pager load drops—capacity and policy teams receive scoped tickets with mix evidence, audit-friendly history stays in Splunk longer than Nexus Dashboard UI defaults, and cross-domain calendars from virtualization TAs reduce false handoffs during maintenance.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest five sourcetypes on index=cisco_dc with Splunkbase 1546 hitting /sedgeapi/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/alerts and .../telemetry/anomalies alongside existing NDFC REST and syslog paths. (2) Install Splunkbase 7777 macros; optionally add 4022/4856/1810 for CMDB overlays. (3) Schedule ndi_noise_category_mix_core with lookup-backed suppression for approved Nexus Dashboard upgrades.

## Evidence

Saved search ndi_noise_category_mix_core, dashboard panel DC_NDI_CategoryMix_18_4_12, weekly CSV exports of mix_pct and z_like archived under network_operations with 13-month retention.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**

- **NDI** 6.3+ should stream Insights telemetry into Splunk; confirm **Nexus Dashboard** RBAC can read **/sedgeapi/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/alerts** and **/sedgeapi/v1/cisco-nir/api/api/telemetry/anomalies** before **Splunk REST Modular Input** accounts poll JSON.
- Install **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (Splunkbase **7777**) so macros align with **cisco:ndi:alerts**, **cisco:ndi:anomalies**, **cisco:ndfc:fabric_status**, **cisco:ndfc:audit**, and **cisco:ndfc:syslog** on **index=cisco_dc** or **network_operations** shards.
- Deploy **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (Splunkbase **1546**) on heavy forwarders with OAuth or service tokens to the nd-cluster FQDN under **/sedgeapi/v1/**; use 60–300s cadences to avoid **HTTP 429** storms while keeping hourly mix math fresh.
- Optional: Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI (**4022**), VMware NSX (**4856**), and VMware vSphere (**1810**) add maintenance calendars when environmental or capacity spikes align with vMotion rather than NX-OS defects.
- Route **cisco:ndfc:syslog** through governance if operator banners may contain sensitive remarks.
- This guide does not replace **UC-18.4.11** collector drop forensics, does not license Advanced Telemetry entitlements, and does not cover third-party AIOps consoles.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

(1) **cisco:ndi:alerts** — Normalize REST fields **alert_category**, **rule_category**, **severity**, **fabric_name**, **site_name**, **alert_id**, **state** via **FIELDALIAS**; expect hundreds to a few thousand events per fabric per day during busy scoring windows.
(2) **cisco:ndi:anomalies** — Preserve **anomaly_id**, **object_name**, **recommendation**, **first_seen**, **last_seen**, and vendor severity in UTC so hourly buckets align with **cisco:ndfc:audit** timestamps.
(3) **cisco:ndfc:fabric_status** — Track LAN inventory reachability; carry **fabric_id** into props so SPL can coalesce **fabric_key** for drilldowns.
(4) **cisco:ndfc:audit** — Capture config-preview rows, policy publish jobs, and workflow IDs; bursts here often precede configuration or change category Insights chatter without hardware failure.
(5) **cisco:ndfc:syslog** — Include facilities mentioning telemetry gRPC stalls, HA failovers, certificate expiry, or worker recycle lines to separate controller churn from fabric-wide regressions.
(6) After playbooks stabilize, avoid duplicate GET fan-out—HTTP 401 signals token drift; 429 responses mean widen poll intervals until Cisco TAC approves aggressive pulls during incidents.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

**Understanding this SPL:** **lane** tags NDI versus NDFC streams; **category_norm** maps vendor wording into compliance, capacity, configuration, change, connectivity, and environmental families. **mix_pct** measures how much each family contributes inside an hour, while **ndi_hr_total**, **z_like**, and **surge** highlight correlated bursts. **fabric_pressure** contrasts controller-side syslog and audit volume with Insights counts to catch automation side effects.

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:ndi:alerts" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:anomalies" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog")
earliest=-14d@m
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval lane=case(
    match(st,"ndi:alerts"),"ndi_alerts",
    match(st,"ndi:anomalies"),"ndi_anomalies",
    match(st,"fabric_status"),"ndfc_fabric",
    match(st,"ndfc:audit"),"ndfc_audit",
    match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),"ndfc_syslog",
    1=1,"other")
| eval is_ndi=if(lane IN ("ndi_alerts","ndi_anomalies"),1,0)
| eval cat_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(alert_category,category,Category,anomaly_category,anomalyCategory,insight_type,rule_category,family,subcategory,insight_family)))
| eval category_norm=case(
    match(cat_raw,"compli|policy|violation|contract"),"compliance",
    match(cat_raw,"capac|util|resource|headroom|queue|congest|drop|buffer|pfc"),"capacity",
    match(cat_raw,"config|drift|consistency|template|golden"),"configuration",
    match(cat_raw,"change|publish|commit|deploy|workflow|preview|job"),"change",
    match(cat_raw,"connect|reach|path|bgp|evpn|vpc|crc|flap|loss|latency|mtu|bfd"),"connectivity",
    match(cat_raw,"power|fan|temp|thermal|environment|cooling|humid|psu"),"environmental",
    isnull(cat_raw) OR cat_raw=="","uncategorized",
    1=1,"general_ndi")
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,nd_fabric,site_name,siteName,inventory_fabric,fabric_id)))
| eval alert_id=trim(coalesce(alert_id,alertId,insight_id,InsightId,anomaly_id,anomalyId,ndi_guid,guid,ruleId,rule_id,signature_id,event_uuid,primary_key)))
| bin _time span=1h
| eval hr=_time
| eval bucket_cat=if(is_ndi=1,category_norm,lane)
| stats count as evt dc(alert_id) as uniq_keys dc(fabric_key) as fabric_card values(lane) as lane_list by hr bucket_cat is_ndi
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_ndi=1,evt,0))) as ndi_hr_total by hr
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_ndi=0,evt,0))) as ndfc_hr_total by hr
| where is_ndi=1
| eval mix_pct=round(100*evt/nullif(ndi_hr_total,0),2)
| eventstats median(ndi_hr_total) as ndi_baseline_med
| eventstats stdev(ndi_hr_total) as ndi_baseline_sd
| eval z_like=round((ndi_hr_total-ndi_baseline_med)/nullif(ndi_baseline_sd,0),2)
| eval surge=if(ndi_hr_total>=1.65*ndi_baseline_med AND ndi_baseline_med>25,1,0)
| where evt>=12 OR mix_pct>=32 OR z_like>=2.2 OR surge=1
| eval fabric_pressure=round(100*ndfc_hr_total/nullif(ndfc_hr_total+ndi_hr_total,0),1)
| sort -ndi_hr_total -mix_pct -evt
| table hr bucket_cat evt uniq_keys fabric_card lane_list mix_pct ndi_hr_total ndfc_hr_total ndi_baseline_med ndi_baseline_sd z_like surge fabric_pressure
| head 400
```

**Pipeline walkthrough** — The search unions five sourcetypes, normalizes categories with **match()** guards, bins **_time** to one hour, and **stats** counts plus distinct **alert_id** values per **bucket_cat**. **eventstats** rebuilds **ndi_hr_total** and **ndfc_hr_total** without **join** commands, then filters dominant rows worth review.

**Tuning** — Raise **evt** threshold in quiet labs; lower **mix_pct** if policy teams need earlier imbalance warnings; widen bin span to 4h on low-rate fabrics.

**Step 3 — Validate**

- Compare **ndi_hr_total** with Nexus Dashboard Insights alert totals for the same hour; double-digit variance often means FIELDALIAS drift or duplicated HEC acknowledgements.
- Spot-check several **alert_id** values in the **NDI** UI; Resources maps to capacity-family normalization, Compliance maps to compliance, Connectivity maps to connectivity, and Operations may land in change or configuration depending on payload wording.
- **timechart** sum(**evt**) by **sourcetype**: flat **cisco:ndi:alerts** with rising **cisco:ndfc:audit** suggests operator churn, not necessarily optic faults.
- Match fabric cardinality to **NDFC** inventory exports; unknown fabrics frequently trace to stale collector VIP DNS.
- Authenticate like input 1546 and call /telemetry/anomalies?limit=5 to confirm JSON keys match indexed fields in verbose mode.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

- Executive stack: hourly **ndi_hr_total** with splits on **bucket_cat** for capacity reviews.
- NOC tables sort **z_like**, passing **fabric_key** and **bucket_cat** as tokens into raw saved searches.
- Automation can open priority-2 tickets when surge=1 and **fabric_pressure** exceeds 35; use email digest for mix shifts without surge.
- Runbooks should link Cisco recommended actions plus internal optics SOP whenever connectivity dominates the mix.
- RBAC: segregate cisco:ndi:* sourcetypes from **cisco:ndfc:audit** readers when audit payloads include credential fingerprints.

**Step 5 — Visualization, alerting, and troubleshooting**

**Visualization** — (1) Timechart **ndi_hr_total** with a baseline band from **ndi_baseline_med**. (2) Stacked bar of **mix_pct** by **bucket_cat**. (3) Scatter **z_like** versus **fabric_pressure** for automation gating. (4) Single-value sparkline for alerts-per-hour SLAs.

**Alerting** — Save as **ndi_noise_category_mix_core** on a 30–60m schedule; throttle 15m per **fabric_key**. Page fabric anchors when connectivity-heavy surge fires; route compliance-heavy **mix_pct** spikes to policy queues with CSV evidence attachments.

**Troubleshooting** — Missing **cisco:ndi:alerts** events: validate **External Streaming** targets, TLS trust to **HEC**, and TCP/443 from forwarders to the cluster VIP. Empty **category_norm**: extend **props.conf** **EVAL** aliases. UI storms but **z_like** stays flat: **alert_id** may be absent—hash _raw for dedup. Surging **ndfc_hr_total** alone: wrong index filter or stalled NDI inputs. HTTP 403 from modular input 1546: refresh IdP group claims. Recycle syslog lines without sustained surge: extend baseline window toward 21d on seasonal campuses. When Cisco publishes new Insight categories, update match() clauses before assuming uncategorized rows indicate data loss.

**Field hygiene** — Track alertCategory, ruleFamily, insightCategory, siteLabel, fabricLabel, workflowId, publishJobId, grpcStatus, haRole, maintenanceTag, pollIntervalSec, streamOffset, certExpEpoch, ndWorkerId, and sourcetypeOverride in version-controlled props so Cisco JSON drift does not break this search.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_dc (sourcetype="cisco:ndi:alerts" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:anomalies" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:fabric_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:audit" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:syslog")
earliest=-14d@m
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval lane=case(
    match(st,"ndi:alerts"),"ndi_alerts",
    match(st,"ndi:anomalies"),"ndi_anomalies",
    match(st,"fabric_status"),"ndfc_fabric",
    match(st,"ndfc:audit"),"ndfc_audit",
    match(st,"ndfc:syslog"),"ndfc_syslog",
    1=1,"other")
| eval is_ndi=if(lane IN ("ndi_alerts","ndi_anomalies"),1,0)
| eval cat_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(alert_category,category,Category,anomaly_category,anomalyCategory,insight_type,rule_category,family,subcategory,insight_family)))
| eval category_norm=case(
    match(cat_raw,"compli|policy|violation|contract"),"compliance",
    match(cat_raw,"capac|util|resource|headroom|queue|congest|drop|buffer|pfc"),"capacity",
    match(cat_raw,"config|drift|consistency|template|golden"),"configuration",
    match(cat_raw,"change|publish|commit|deploy|workflow|preview|job"),"change",
    match(cat_raw,"connect|reach|path|bgp|evpn|vpc|crc|flap|loss|latency|mtu|bfd"),"connectivity",
    match(cat_raw,"power|fan|temp|thermal|environment|cooling|humid|psu"),"environmental",
    isnull(cat_raw) OR cat_raw=="","uncategorized",
    1=1,"general_ndi")
| eval fabric_key=trim(lower(coalesce(fabric_name,fabricName,fabric,nd_fabric,site_name,siteName,inventory_fabric,fabric_id)))
| eval alert_id=trim(coalesce(alert_id,alertId,insight_id,InsightId,anomaly_id,anomalyId,ndi_guid,guid,ruleId,rule_id,signature_id,event_uuid,primary_key)))
| bin _time span=1h
| eval hr=_time
| eval bucket_cat=if(is_ndi=1,category_norm,lane)
| stats count as evt dc(alert_id) as uniq_keys dc(fabric_key) as fabric_card values(lane) as lane_list by hr bucket_cat is_ndi
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_ndi=1,evt,0))) as ndi_hr_total by hr
| eventstats sum(eval(if(is_ndi=0,evt,0))) as ndfc_hr_total by hr
| where is_ndi=1
| eval mix_pct=round(100*evt/nullif(ndi_hr_total,0),2)
| eventstats median(ndi_hr_total) as ndi_baseline_med
| eventstats stdev(ndi_hr_total) as ndi_baseline_sd
| eval z_like=round((ndi_hr_total-ndi_baseline_med)/nullif(ndi_baseline_sd,0),2)
| eval surge=if(ndi_hr_total>=1.65*ndi_baseline_med AND ndi_baseline_med>25,1,0)
| where evt>=12 OR mix_pct>=32 OR z_like>=2.2 OR surge=1
| eval fabric_pressure=round(100*ndfc_hr_total/nullif(ndfc_hr_total+ndi_hr_total,0),1)
| sort -ndi_hr_total -mix_pct -evt
| table hr bucket_cat evt uniq_keys fabric_card lane_list mix_pct ndi_hr_total ndfc_hr_total ndi_baseline_med ndi_baseline_sd z_like surge fabric_pressure
| head 400
```

## Visualization

(1) Timechart ndi_hr_total with reference band from ndi_baseline_med. (2) Stacked bar mix_pct by bucket_cat. (3) Table of fabric_key, bucket_cat, z_like, surge for NOC. (4) Scatter fabric_pressure vs z_like for automation gating. (5) Alert workflow: surge=1 to PagerDuty, high mix_pct without surge to email digest with drilldown to raw cisco:ndi:alerts.

## Known False Positives

**ndi_duplicate_fanout** HEC or REST collectors occasionally ingest the same logical Insight twice when two forwarders target the same token; deduplicate on alert_id before trusting mix_pct, then retire the duplicate input once ack paths converge.

**controller_upgrade_wave** Rolling Nexus Dashboard upgrades reshuffle worker partitions so Insights categories wobble for a few hours without leaf failures; require surge persisting beyond six hours or corroborating CRC syslog from NX-OS before paging hardware teams.

**fabric_inventory_resync** Large LAN automation reconcile jobs spike cisco:ndfc:fabric_status counts and inflate fabric_pressure while NDI volume stays normal; ignore hardware escalations unless cisco:ndi:alerts evt grows in the same buckets.

**compliance_scan_replay** Weekly policy rescans replay historical noncompliant rows and temporarily lift compliance mix_pct; compare uniq_keys to active rule IDs shown in the NDI compliance workspace before opening tickets.

**environmental_sensor_glitch** Brief PSU or thermal telemetry glitches can tag environmental spikes that clear within two polls; demand z_like above 2.2 plus SNMP evidence from NDFC before dispatching field services.

**aci_vmware_calendar_noise** Splunkbase 4022, 4856, and 1810 maintenance windows sometimes align temporally with Insights surges even when NX-OS is healthy; confirm CMDB change tags before claiming cross-domain root cause.

**api_rate_limit_blip** Short HTTP 429 responses from /telemetry/alerts shrink ingest without indicating fabric outages; inspect splunkd.log on 1546 hosts, increase intervals, and only escalate if drop counters on collectors also rise per UC-18.4.11 guidance.

**symmetric_link_event** A single VPC or MLAG regression can fan out dozens of connectivity-family Insights tied to one member port; use fabric_key plus interface tuples in drilldowns so mix_pct storms do not automatically imply multi-site outages.
